rulings.Case Title: CENTER FOR GENETICS AND SOCIETY ET AL VS. XAVIER BECERRA ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion calendar for Friday, December 20, 2024, Line 1. PETITIONERS CENTER FOR GENETICS AND SOCIETY, EQUAL JUSTICE SOCIETY, AND PETE SHANKS' Motion For Judgment. Hearing required. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/VH)

rulings.Case Title: ANGIE MILON VS. JOHN MUNGUIA ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion calendar for Friday, December 20, 2024, Line 3. CROSS DEFENDANT GEORGE BENETATOS' Amended DEMURRER to Amended CROSS COMPLAINT. Cross-defendant Benetatos's demurrer to the Second Amended Cross-Complaint ("SACC") is sustained with twenty days leave to amend. Despite cross-complaints' dismissal of causes of action two and fourteen for aiding and abetting underlying torts, the SACC still alleges claims that implicate Civil Code section 1714.10 [attorney-client conspiracy; court order]. (See also Cortese v. Sherwood (2018) 26 Cal.App.5th 445, 455 [the word "conspiracy" has no talismanic significance to implicate Civil Code section 1714.10]; Howard v. Superior Court (1992) 2 Cal.App.4th 745, 749 [aiding and abetting allegations trigger application of Civil Code section 1714.20.) In this case, the SACC continues to have numerous "conspiracy" allegations that are incorporated by reference in all of the claims. (See, e.g., SACC, pars. 25 [Benetatos counseled Riedel and Milon to violate an agreement and court orders]; 38 [Milon and Benetatos jointly concealed Riedel's incapacity]; 43-44 [Benetatos subordinated partnership interests in favor of Milon's; reference to "scheme"].) Cross-complainants have leave to delete the "conspiracy" allegations and/or clearly allege claims that fall under the exceptions to the pre-filing order requirement. (See Civil Code sec. 1714.10(c).) The court has no opinion on whether the proposed Third Amended Complaint cures the cited defects. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/VH)

rulings.Case Title: **Transferred to San Diego County** ANN WEST VS. MANOUSO MANOS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: First Amended Notice Of Motion And Motion To Stay Pursuant To C.C.P. Section 918.5
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion calendar for Friday, December 20, 2024, Line 4. 3 - PLAINTIFF ANN WEST's Motion To Stay Pursuant To C.C.P. Section 918.5. Plaintiff's motion to stay per CCP 918.5 is DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. CCP 918.5(a) gives the trial court the discretion to "stay the enforcement of a judgment or order." One factor in exercising its discretion is the amount of the creditor's judgment or order. (CCP 918.5(b)(2).) Here, there is presently no fee order for the court to stay and the amount of fees is therefore unknown. This motion is premature. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/VH)
